reasoning: The valuation is genuinely cheap for a company still growing revenue at ~16% and sitting at 21x earnings vs 38x sector peers. The March 23 product-driven surge shows the market still has appetite for SN's innovation story. But the macro environment is hostile and growth is decelerating, so aggressive addition is not warranted. The position size of ~8% is appropriate. HOLD with a clear trigger: if price reclaims the 200-day MA (~$109) with volume, that's a signal to add. If $100 support breaks, reduce. Do not sit passively on a declining price with no plan. catalysts.description: Q1 2026 earnings — guidance will confirm whether 16% growth is the new floor or if further deceleration is coming

bullCase: SharkNinja trades at a 45% valuation discount to the consumer appliances sector (P/E ~21x vs 38x sector), with analyst consensus price target of $132-141 implying ~30% upside. Revenue still growing double-digits at ~16% YoY. March 23 product launch surge (+7.25%) shows the market still responds to innovation. Stronger USD is a currency tailwind for ILS investors. Short interest declining. No fundamental breakdown — growth is decelerating, not collapsing.
bearCase: Revenue growth decelerated sharply from 30% to 16% YoY. Macro environment is hostile — risk-off, high oil, elevated rates, geopolitical uncertainty all pressure consumer discretionary. Stock is below all major moving averages (20, 50, 200-day MA) in a clear downtrend. Previous analyst target of $149 was never reached; current targets may prove optimistic if growth continues to slow. USD/ILS at 3.65-3.75 is good for conversion but the underlying USD strength reflects economic uncertainty.
